data.title: Australian Shares Flat as Middle East Tensions Escalate; Accent Posts Lower Like-for-Like Sales for 23 Weeks to June 8
data.description:
Australian shares ended flat with a negative bias on Friday after Israel attacked Iran's nuclear program and ballistic-missile sites, hurting investor sentiment.

The S&P/ASX 200 Index was little changed to close at 8,547.40.

The strikes on Iran threaten to drag the Middle East further into turmoil, with potential repercussions for the global economy, Bloomberg reported.

The attack is "poised to echo through global markets - not just as a geopolitical flashpoint, but more as a stark wake-up call," said Hebe Chen, an analyst at Vantage Markets in Melbourne.

On the domestic front, the Reserve Bank of Australia (RBA) expects a volatile bond market amid a constantly changing global economy and has implemented a fresh monetary policy framework to ensure liquidity and market stability, David Jacobs, head of domestic markets at the RBA, said.

Australia's expected trimmed mean inflation rate rose by 0.9 percentage points to 5% in June, according to a report from the Melbourne Institute.

The RBA is expected to hold rates steady in July, cut them by 25 basis points in August and November, followed by two additional cuts in early 2026, lowering the cash rate to 2.85% from 3.85%, according to a Thursday report by Westpac .

In corporate news, Accent Group reported a 1% drop in like-for-like sales for the 23 weeks to June 8, citing low overall growth in the lifestyle footwear market from March to early June. Shares of the company fell 25% at market close and earlier hit a near-three-year low.

Rio Tinto Group deployed iManage, a platform used to manage documents and other business content, as part of its broader strategy to modernize its legal operations. Shares of the company fell past 1% at market close.

Woodside Energy Group and its North West Shelf joint venture agreed to extend the consultation period for the North West Shelf LNG project in Western Australia regarding the government's proposed conditions as part of the project extension environmental approval process. Shares of the company rose past 7%

at market close.

data.title: Japan Shares Slip as Middle East Tensions Rise After Israeli Strike on Iran
data.description:
Japanese shares ended in the red on Friday as an Israeli attack heightened Middle East tensions, adding to global market pressure already stirred by US President Donald Trump's trade policy shifts.

The Nikkei 225 fell 0.89%, or 338.84 points, to end at 37,834.25.

Israel declared a state of emergency ahead of an expected missile and drone strike from Tehran, following what it described as a preemptive move targeting Iran's nuclear program. Explosions were reported near Tehran early Friday, state-run Nour News said.

Two US officials said Israel had begun strikes on Iran without US involvement. CNN reported that Trump was holding a cabinet meeting.

The attack follows stalled US-Iran nuclear talks, with a sixth round set for Sunday in Oman, according to officials from both sides and Omani mediators.

On the economic front, Japan's industrial production fell 1.1% in April from the previous month, with inventories down 0.8% and shipments flat, government data showed on Friday. The operating ratio rose 1.3%, while production capacity dipped 0.4%.

Service sector activity edged up 0.3%, lifted by gains in finance, IT, and wholesale trade, though transport and postal services dropped 3.4%. The unadjusted index was up 1.9% on the year.

In corporate updates, Yamada Holdings expects 28.44 billion yen in dividends from Yamada Denki on June 30. The income will be booked in non-consolidated results for FY2026, with no impact on group earnings.

Toho will acquire Sankyo Shokucho, formed from six Sankyo Group firms, for about 404 million yen. The deal supports Toho's poultry expansion under its SHIFT-UP 2027 strategy and is expected to close by early October.

Renova sold 277.2 million kWh of electricity in May, up 50.4% on strong biomass output. Year-to-date sales rose 145.2% to 540.8 million kWh.

data.title: Tap Global to List on London's Junior AIM
data.description:
By Anthony O. Goriainoff

Tap Global said it plans to list on London's junior AIM later in the month.

The regulated cryptocurrency app said Friday that it wasn't raising any new capital nor would it be providing a secondary offering as part of the admission. The company said it had 743.4 million ordinary shares in issue at present.

The board said that AIM was a more appropriate market for the company, allowing it to attract a wider set of investors and improve liquidity over time.

The company said it expects its admission will become effective on June 27, and that its shares will stop trading on the Aquis Stock Exchange on that date.

The company said that Spark Advisory Partners is its nominated adviser and that Tennyson Securities will continue to act as the company's broker after admission.

"The board believes AIM is the right platform for a company of our scale and ambition, providing the profile and access to a wider investor base that will support our growth," Chief Executive Arsen Torosian said.

Write to Anthony O. Goriainoff at anthony.orunagoriainoff@dowjones.com
